plan.reason: To provide personalized guitar buying tips, need user's experience, music style, and budget.

p5_candidate.answer: When shopping for a new guitar, consider the following: 1) Body shape and construction (e.g., single-cut vs. double-cut, bolt-on vs. set neck). 2) Pickup type (single-coil vs. humbucker) and electronics (e.g., coil-splitting for versatility). 3) Neck profile and scale length for playability. 4) Weight and balance. 5) Tone characteristics (bright vs. warm). 6) Budget and brand reputation. Try out different models to see what feels and sounds best for your style.
p5_candidate.reasoning_summary: The user asks for tips on what to look for in a new guitar. From memory m_ed03a790beea6aa98426 and source 134, the assistant provided detailed differences between Stratocaster and Les Paul, covering body, pickups, neck, tone, etc. From memory m_e782ef1e40aceb381bd9 and source 135, the assistant listed genres suited to Les Paul, indicating tonal versatility. These are used to compile general buying tips.
